scores.correctness_notes: All four fields (interface, limit, optional, scope) are correctly described with accurate types, defaults, and descriptions. The three role keys (provides, requires, peers) are correctly explained.
scores.hallucination_notes: The note 'Every charm implicitly gets a built-in provides: juju-info endpoint' is not present in the gold standard and may be an extrapolation/hallucination. The note about 'Charmcraft 2.5' and auto-generation of metadata.yaml is also not in the gold standard. The gold standard notes that interface 'cannot be juju or start with juju-' and uses 'only a-z and -' — this constraint is omitted. The gold standard also notes optional 'should always be explicitly included other than for peers' — this nuance is missing.
